_meta.location: Load-bearing fog oracle; moved from tools/spikes/fog by rebrand W5.
_meta.source: §6.4 fog/lighting calibration, 2026-07-07, DF 53.15 Steam, fort Sazirzes/Bridgedbranded
_meta.oracle: lossless capture-tiledump ground_truth.png (native DF render); NOT the JPEG path
_meta.consumer: WebGL FogParams UBO (webgl-render-report §D) / canvas client constants (dwf-tiles.js §3.4)

seeDown.correction: 2026-07-07 sweep #2 (verification-sweep-2 report): The owner reported real bluish depth fog in the actual DF window (elevation ~48, this fort's window_z=164) that the 'identity' verdict below missed -- because /frame.jpg and /tiledump both read a pre-present framebuffer, and DF applies this tint in its screen-space present pass, after both oracles' read point. Verified live: PrintWindow capture of the real window vs /frame.jpg vs /tiledump at the SAME camera (53,75,164) -- real window shows a fully-covered blue-teal valley; /frame.jpg is almost entirely black there; /tiledump shows correct void + unfogged tree-canopy slices only. Both oracles are fog-blind for multi-level scenes (a second, independent gap from the fictional-JPEG-fog finding below). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-fog-lighting-report.md §7 for full method/fit.
seeDown.fogColorNote: empirical asymptotic color (alpha->1); within ~10-14/255 of the raws MULTILEVEL_OVERLAY/MULTILEVEL_AIR sheet color (89,146,172,alpha96) noted as unused in the old evidence below -- circumstantial evidence that sheet IS what the present pass applies
seeDown.alphaByDepthNote: depth = camera_z - local_ground_z. Concave/saturating curve, NOT linear through origin -- fit jointly (alternating least squares) across 2 materials (grass base (78,104,52), stone rubble base (110,104,103)) x 3 channels x 10 depths; both materials converge to the same asymptotic color, confirming a material-independent screen-space overlay (not a per-sprite recolor). Mean abs residual 2.85/255.
seeDown.linearRateApproxNote: for WB-10 GL hook's single-scalar seeDownTint.a (amt=clamp(depth*rate,0,1)); matches the measured curve well for depth>=5 (where the visual complaint concentrates), undershoots depth 1-3 by ~0.15-0.2 alpha. A depth-indexed LUT (WB-10 already packs a 4-bit depth into the instance attr) would track the curve better -- follow-up, shader change, out of this sweep's docs/JSON-only scope.
seeDown.seeDownTintUBOvalueNote: [r,g,b,rate] normalized 0-1 (fogColorRgb/255 + linearRateApprox), drop-in for RenderParams.seeDownTint (web/js/dwf-gl.js WB-10 hook) once an executor is ready to wire it -- NOT wired by this sweep (docs/JSON only, no product-code change).

shaderLinearFit._meta: WIRED 2026-07-07 (WB-4 fog completion executor): the single-scalar linearRateApprox (0.15) undershoots depth 1-3 by ~0.15-0.2 alpha per this sweep's own note. A least-squares line fit ONLY through the 7 measured non-clipped points (depth 1..7 from alphaByDepth above -- depth 0 is never fogged, depth 8/9 are the curve's own clip-to-1.0) tracks the SAME measured curve far better: mean abs residual ~0.002 alpha (~0.5/255), max resid ~0.004 alpha, vs. the single-rate approximation's much larger low-depth error. This is a re-expression of the SAME fit, not a new curve -- alpha(depth) = clamp(intercept + depth*rate, 0, 1) for depth>=1, else 0.
shaderLinearFit.fitMethod: ordinary least squares, y=alphaByDepth[d] vs x=d, for d in 1..7 (8/9 excluded -- they're the measured curve's own clip region, not informative for the line's slope/intercept)
wiredInto: web/js/dwf-gl.js (RenderParams.seeDownCurve.x + seeDownTint.a in the fragment shader's seeDownDepth branch; same constants duplicated for the JS-side building/unit z-fade belowAlpha helper)
shaderLinearFit.notWired: seeAbove/indoor: NOT part of this re-expression -- those verdicts (seeAbove: delete, indoor: identity/remove) are unrelated to the seeDown curve shape and were executed as straight deletions per their own unchanged evidence above, not curve-fitted.

seeDown.method: OS-level PrintWindow screenshot of the live DF window (passive, no DF_LOCK for the screenshot) vs /frame.jpg vs /tiledump(gt=1,atlas=0) at the same camera; camera x,y fixed at (53,75), window_z swept 161(depth0)..170(depth9) and restored to the original 160; 3 fixed screen boxes per material (grass, stone rubble), median RGB per box per depth.

supersededVerdict.evidence: 108 valley floors x cameras z160..z164 pixel-identical (mean|delta|=0.0); flat-ground sweep d0..d10: median tile delta 0.0, frames z165..z171 bit-identical
supersededVerdict.note: DF's internal capture paths (both /frame.jpg's composite_seedown_into and /tiledump) render see-down substitution at FULL brightness -- this part is STILL TRUE, re-confirmed in sweep #2. What this verdict got wrong: it concluded from that that DF itself has no see-down fog. It does -- the fog is applied in the present pass, downstream of where both capture paths read. The JPEG oracle's composite_seedown_into (0.74-0.14(d-1), x0.90G x0.74R) is still fictional/wrong-shaped fog and should still be neutralized or scored against the raw tiledump oracle for CONTENT purposes -- but see the corrected top-level entry for the tint DF's screen actually shows.

seeAbove.evidence: top_shadow, signpost, tree_plus_one, shadow_tree layers zero in ALL scenes incl. forest and elevated cameras; canopy renders only as camera-plane slices
seeAbove.note: DF draws NO above-camera translucent canopy. Delete alphaAbove/brightnessAbove canopy pass (it adds false content).

indoor.evidence: 158 subterranean floors: rendered/sprite ratio (1.009,1.0,1.0); A/B on all-dug scene: removing today's rgba(8,10,34,0.17) wash improves MAE 16.71->13.78
indoor.note: REMOVE the indoor/underground ambient wash entirely. DF applies no indoor darkening.

sunlitTint.evidence: rendered grass +5,+4,-1 vs its own atlas sprite; water/stone/soil identity in same frame; per-pixel diff structured (std~15) -> runtime seasonal grass recolor, not a lighting pass
sunlitTint.note: GrassLight and GrassDark render IDENTICALLY (same texpos, same color) - drop the client's per-state tint split; calibrate the single summer grass wash to hit (78,104,52). Seasonal palettes = sprite-level recolor keyed on season (coverage agent: grass raws seasonal colors).

hidden.evidence: all hidden tiles render texpos 2566 == HIDDEN_ROCK cell, identity (380 undesignated hidden tiles median exactly (49,44,52))
hidden.note: Replace HIDDEN_COLOR [6,6,8] flat fill with the opaque hatch sprite. True void (no tile) stays black.

designationHighlight.evidence: 154 designated hidden tiles: rendered - sprite = (+27,+29,+26) under the pick icon
designationHighlight.note: DF lightens designated tiles ~+27/255 plus the designation icon sprite. Feed to designation rendering (coverage agent).

wallShadows.sheet: shadows_wall.png (5x5, 16 tokens WALL_SHADOW_{STRAIGHT_|NEAR_|AT_CORNER_}*), shadows_ramp.png (9x9, RAMP_SHADOW_*), vision_shadows.png (4x5, 17 VISION_SHADOW_* fog-of-war edges)
wallShadows.mask: shadow_flag bits 2..9 = wall-at N,S,W,E,NW,NE,SW,SE (decoded this session); equivalently derivable client-side from world-cache 8-neighbor wall adjacency
wallShadows.evidence: floors with N-wall bit show top-edge gradient x0.75->x0.96 vs clean floors flat; render-buffer verdict's shadow_flag_ratios.json agrees (blue-tinted, R darkest)
wallShadows.note: This IS DF's per-tile 'lighting': directional blue-black gradient decals at wall bases + ramp shadows + vision-edge shadows. Exact via sprite blits keyed by the 8-bit adjacency mask (WebGL ingest joinMask). Token-level mask->cell table needs one controlled-scene pass (5-min tiledump study).

dayNight.evidence: 24 captures across 10,682 ticks (~9 in-game days, all times of day): scene mean range < 0.9/255
dayNight.note: No diurnal tint exists. Do not add time-of-day to the wire for rendering.

liquids.sheet: liquids.png col0 water col1 magma, row = 7 - depth, authored alpha (255,255,255,255,230,204,179 for d7..d1)
liquids.depthSource: designation.flow_size (wire 'flow'); render-buffer liquid_flag low byte = 0x20|(depth<<2), shore/foam high bits
liquids.evidence: camera-plane pond (d7) renders as the raw depth-7 cell + wave animation (measured (1,86,100) vs cell (0,79,91)); d1-below-camera water unfogged
liquids.note: No extra tint. Client bug is draw-path (flat waterRgb fill bleeding / wrong stage), not calibration. Foam/wave sprites are runtime-generated (null atlas slots) - animation channel work.

variation.evidence: texpos is a pure function of (ttname[, base material]) in every scene tested (e.g. SoilFloor1..4 -> 17241/17517/17526/17535, GrassXFloor1..4 -> 24123..26); 100.000% stable across ticks and camera shifts (render-buffer verdict D)
variation.note: No per-(x,y,z) render hash for terrain. Fix = token map must preserve the ttname variant digit (today it collapses 101 stone-floor ttnames to STONE_FLOOR_5). Sweep table (11s/z-level) available as oracle/backstop.
